title: PAPUC Commissioner Zerfuss Leads National Resolution Recognizing RNG as Strategic Resource
content: Pennsylvania Public Utility Commission (PUC) Commissioner Kathryn L. Zerfuss championed a national resolution recognizing Renewable Natural Gas (RNG) as a strategic energy resource, which was adopted by the National Association of Regulatory Utility Commissioners (NARUC) at its 2026 Winter Policy Summit in Washington, D.C. The resolution highlights RNG, derived from agricultural, municipal, and industrial waste, as a critical tool for meeting growing energy demand while ensuring system reliability and reducing emissions. It calls for clear regulatory frameworks to support RNG development, including cost recovery mechanisms, market-based procurement, and interconnection standards. Commissioner Zerfuss emphasized an "all-of-the-above" energy strategy, noting that RNG leverages existing infrastructure to deliver economic and environmental benefits. The move underscores the increasing focus of state regulators on integrating lower-carbon resources into long-term utility planning.
author: Analysts Account
